title: The Gang Cracks the Liberty Bell

jokes.text: He really has an ugly face. He is certainly ugly. But, you know what, that's not really what bothers me about this. What bothers me is that he looks like an old woman.
jokes.setup: Characters criticizing someone's appearance in a painting
jokes.punchline: The specific complaint that he looks like an old woman
jokes.explanation: Basic setup establishing the gang's shallow criticism of George Washington's portrait

dialogue.line: He really has an ugly face. He is certainly ugly. But, you know what, that's not really what bothers me about this. What bothers me is that he looks like an old woman.

jokes.text: I mean, look at Ronald Reagan, right?
jokes.setup: Claim that many presidents end up looking like women
jokes.punchline: Using Ronald Reagan as an example of a president who looks like a woman
jokes.explanation: Unexpected Reagan reference is absurd and economical

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I mean, look at Ronald Reagan, right?

jokes.text: we got to suffer just because some old dude who looks like Meryl Streep chopped down a cherry tree, like, ten million years ago?
jokes.setup: Complaining about having to look at Washington's portrait
jokes.punchline: Meryl Streep comparison and wildly inaccurate 'ten million years ago'
jokes.explanation: Charlie's typical absurd historical ignorance combined with specific celebrity comparison

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: we got to suffer just because some old dude who looks like Meryl Streep chopped down a cherry tree, like, ten million years ago?

jokes.text: Well, that proves our point, man. We're not the first people to try and take that thing down.
jokes.setup: Portrait is bolted to the wall
jokes.punchline: Mac's logical conclusion that others have tried to remove it
jokes.explanation: Mac's simple logic applied to their vandalism attempt

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, that proves our point, man. We're not the first people to try and take that thing down.

jokes.text: they can come by, suck up some history, do some shots, get blasted.
jokes.setup: Describing tourists visiting their historical bar
jokes.punchline: Transitioning from educational tourism to getting drunk
jokes.explanation: Shows the gang's commercial motivation disguised as historical interest

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: they can come by, suck up some history, do some shots, get blasted.

jokes.text: Well, the year was 1412... No. Really? Let Dennis, let Dennis...
jokes.setup: Charlie starting to tell their historical story
jokes.punchline: Getting the year wildly wrong (1412 vs 1776)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - confident ignorance about basic historical facts

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, the year was 1412... No. Really?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Let Dennis, let Dennis...

jokes.text: I do not give a shit, Deandra. Now, get me a flagon of ale. Get your own ale, dick. You are my slave.
jokes.setup: 1776 setting with Dennis as Dee's 'master'
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal followed by slavery demand and Dee's defiant response
jokes.explanation: Establishes the dark comedy dynamic between Dennis and Dee in period setting

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I do not give a shit, Deandra. Now, get me a flagon of ale.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Get your own ale, dick.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You are my slave.

jokes.text: I saved you from being burned at the stake for being a witch. You're the one who accused me of being a witch in the first place, Dennis.
jokes.setup: Dennis claiming he saved Dee from witch burning
jokes.punchline: Dee revealing he was the one who accused her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis manipulation - creating a problem to solve it and claim credit. Dark but brilliantly structured.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I saved you from being burned at the stake for being a witch.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You're the one who accused me of being a witch in the first place, Dennis.

jokes.text: You know that chick I've been banging down at Independence Hall? Mm, the fat one. She's not that fat. Yeah, she's pretty fat, dude.
jokes.setup: Mac mentioning a woman he's seeing
jokes.punchline: Immediate fat-shaming from friends despite Mac's weak defense
jokes.explanation: Shows the gang's casual cruelty but relies on basic mean humor

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You know that chick I've been banging down at Independence Hall?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Mm, the fat one.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She's not that fat.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, she's pretty fat, dude.

jokes.text: We have to make our own declaration. Yes. Yes, we need to make a declaration of dependence.
jokes.setup: Need to show loyalty to the British
jokes.punchline: Declaration of dependence vs independence
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows their cowardly opportunism perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We have to make our own declaration.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yes, we need to make a declaration of dependence.

jokes.text: Merrily I be able to guide you toward some interest in some purchase.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to speak in period-appropriate language
jokes.punchline: Completely butchering ye olde English syntax
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie - attempting sophistication and failing spectacularly

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Merrily I be able to guide you toward some interest in some purchase.

jokes.text: We're not traders, Charlie. We're thieves. You want his pumpkins, we kill him and take his pumpkins. This is 1776. We'll get away with it!
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to negotiate a trade
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual suggestion of murder justified by the time period
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Mac's violent tendencies rationalized by historical setting

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We're not traders, Charlie. We're thieves. You want his pumpkins, we kill him and take his pumpkins. This is 1776. We'll get away with it!

jokes.text: I can hear you.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie planning to murder him openly
jokes.punchline: Customer's matter-of-fact response to their murder plot
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that makes their incompetence even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: I can hear you.

jokes.text: What? He stole our furs! Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah. You shit head.
jokes.setup: They were planning elaborate murder while customer escapes
jokes.punchline: Realization that victim escaped with their goods during their planning
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of incompetent criminals being outsmarted by their intended victim

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He stole our furs!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You shit head.

jokes.text: I'm dressed like a nobleman, okay? These people respond to royalty.
jokes.setup: Dennis defending his outfit choice
jokes.punchline: His delusional belief about how British class system works
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissism and delusion about his own importance

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm dressed like a nobleman, okay? These people respond to royalty.

jokes.text: This appears to be a rendering of... the two of you sodomizing the king whilst the queen is forced to witness.
jokes.setup: The gang presenting their 'declaration' with illustration
jokes.punchline: Cricket's clinical description of their horrible drawing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly escalated cringe - their attempt at political allegiance becomes graphic sexual assault

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: This appears to be a rendering of... the two of you sodomizing the king whilst the queen is forced to witness.

jokes.text: No, look. I'm holding homeboy's arms back. Dennis is ready to jam him in the face, and the queen is impressed.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to explain their horrific drawing
jokes.punchline: His completely tone-deaf 'clarification' that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Mac's attempt to salvage the situation only makes the sexual violence interpretation more explicit

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, look. I'm holding homeboy's arms back. Dennis is ready to jam him in the face, and the queen is impressed.

jokes.text: We're going to tar and feather the shit out of her when she gets back.
jokes.setup: Dee accidentally spilling ale on Cricket
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual promise of brutal punishment to appease Cricket
jokes.explanation: Shows how quickly the gang will throw each other under the bus

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We're going to tar and feather the shit out of her when she gets back.

jokes.text: you are both slanderous perverts lacking any semblance of moral fortitude. However, I do desire to seek further counsel with the lady.
jokes.setup: Cricket condemning their moral character
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking for help courting Dee despite calling them perverts
jokes.explanation: Cricket's hypocrisy and desperation showing through his moral posturing

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: you are both slanderous perverts lacking any semblance of moral fortitude. However, I do desire to seek further counsel with the lady.

jokes.text: Bro, it's 1776. They don't have any rights. You can just take them and force them to do whatever you want.
jokes.setup: Cricket wanting to court Dee like a gentleman
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual advice to just assault her because of the time period
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Mac's toxic masculinity rationalized by historical context

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Bro, it's 1776. They don't have any rights. You can just take them and force them to do whatever you want.

jokes.text: Do not shoot that pumpkin! What are you doing? I stole a bunch of guns! I'm testing them out, Charles!
jokes.setup: Charlie protecting the pumpkin they acquired
jokes.punchline: Mac shooting at Charlie to test stolen guns
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of Mac's reckless violence and Charlie's misplaced priorities

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Do not shoot that pumpkin! What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I stole a bunch of guns! I'm testing them out, Charles!

jokes.text: The pumpkin's innocent!
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to protect the pumpkin from gunfire
jokes.punchline: Attributing moral innocence to a vegetable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie absurdism - anthropomorphizing objects while ignoring actual danger

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The pumpkin's innocent!

jokes.text: You know what, okay, you guys. I tell you what, you win. I'm a witch, okay? I'm a witch! And I curse both of you and all of your stupid guns.
jokes.setup: Dee being accused of witchcraft
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically admitting to witchcraft and cursing their guns
jokes.explanation: Dee's exasperated sarcasm that the idiots take literally

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You know what, okay, you guys. I tell you what, you win. I'm a witch, okay? I'm a witch! And I curse both of you and all of your stupid guns.

jokes.text: She cursed the gun! She put a curse on the gun! Let's try this one. Nothing!
jokes.setup: Dee sarcastically cursing their guns
jokes.punchline: Charlie and Mac genuinely believing the curse worked when guns jam
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of their stupidity - taking Dee's sarcasm as literal magic

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: She cursed the gun! She put a curse on the gun! Let's try this one. Nothing!

jokes.text: Oh, I got some wooden teeth put in. Yeah. Well, all the patriots are doing it, including Washington, which is why it makes sense, okay, if we're going undercover!
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his appearance choice
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding Washington's dentures as a fashion trend for disguise
jokes.explanation: Dennis's vanity leading to historical misunderstanding and poor spy craft

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, I got some wooden teeth put in. Yeah. Well, all the patriots are doing it, including Washington, which is why it makes sense, okay, if we're going undercover!

jokes.text: I mean, taxation with representation, right?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to blend in with patriots
jokes.punchline: Getting the revolutionary slogan completely backwards
jokes.explanation: Perfect historical ignorance - getting the core revolutionary principle exactly wrong

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I mean, taxation with representation, right?

jokes.text: Hey, fellas, who's taking the minutes here? 'Cause I feel like I could be an asset to you as a secretary, and-- Let's start by taking down all of their names. First and last, please.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to infiltrate the patriots meeting
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking for their names like an obvious spy
jokes.explanation: Mac's terrible espionage skills making him an obvious British informant

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hey, fellas, who's taking the minutes here? 'Cause I feel like I could be an asset to you as a secretary, and-- Let's start by taking down all of their names. First and last, please.

jokes.text: you'd think people who came here so many years before the English, that they'd build bigger houses! Yeah. You'd think they'd have huge homes.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Native American housing
jokes.punchline: Expecting Native Americans to have built massive houses despite different lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Shows their cultural ignorance but relies on basic stereotype humor

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: you'd think people who came here so many years before the English, that they'd build bigger houses!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah. You'd think they'd have huge homes.

jokes.text: I speak English. Oh, shit! For real?
jokes.setup: Charlie and Mac making racist assumptions about Native Americans
jokes.punchline: Native American speaks English, catching them off guard
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of their assumptions, though setup relies on stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Native American
dialogue.line: I speak English.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, shit! For real?

jokes.text: You want me to scalp them?
jokes.setup: Charlie and Mac asking for help with their cursed guns
jokes.punchline: Native American's matter-of-fact offer to scalp them
jokes.explanation: Deadpan violence offer but relies on Native American stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Native American
dialogue.line: You want me to scalp them?

jokes.text: Give me that skunk pelt. I'll use it for hair.
jokes.setup: Mac getting scalped and losing hair
jokes.punchline: Using a skunk pelt as replacement hair
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of Mac's vanity leading to ridiculous appearance fix

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Give me that skunk pelt. I'll use it for hair.

jokes.text: Oh! Yep. Yep. There it is.
jokes.setup: Cricket reminding Dee she spit in his face
jokes.punchline: Dee's casual acknowledgment like it's no big deal
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's casual disregard for Cricket's feelings

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh! Yep. Yep. There it is.

jokes.text: A simple "no" would have sufficed.
jokes.setup: Cricket proposing marriage and Dee spitting in his face again
jokes.punchline: Cricket's understated response to being spat on during proposal
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to extreme rejection - understated and quotable

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: A simple "no" would have sufficed.

jokes.text: you got a revolution coming. And pretty soon, all of the slaves are gonna be free, and I will be owned by no man.
jokes.setup: Dee rejecting Cricket's proposal
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of what the Revolution was about
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's historical ignorance about what the Revolution accomplished

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: you got a revolution coming. And pretty soon, all of the slaves are gonna be free, and I will be owned by no man.

jokes.text: We're gonna have to work up to that, though, 'cause you are crazy ugly.
jokes.setup: Dee suddenly agreeing to marriage for money
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting Cricket's appearance while accepting
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee cruelty - even when accepting a proposal she has to be mean

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: We're gonna have to work up to that, though, 'cause you are crazy ugly.

jokes.text: We're gonna have to work up to that, though, 'cause you are crazy ugly.
jokes.setup: Dee agreeing to marry Cricket enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting his appearance as a condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee moment - agreeing to marriage while being casually cruel, great economy

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: We're gonna have to work up to that, though, 'cause you are crazy ugly.

jokes.text: You want the slave, you gotta pay for the slave. We're not gonna give it to you for free.
jokes.setup: Cricket wanting to marry Dee
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating his sister like property to be sold
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's opportunism and complete lack of familial loyalty

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You want the slave, you gotta pay for the slave. We're not gonna give it to you for free.

jokes.text: Would you like a spot of tea? Yeeees...! Would you like a spot of tea? Ye-es. Draw it out more. Draw it out more than that? Yees...!
jokes.setup: Dennis teaching Mac to speak with British accent
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous over-pronunciation of 'yes' getting increasingly absurd
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of their terrible British accent attempts getting progressively worse

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Would you like a spot of tea?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeeees...!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Would you like a spot of tea?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Ye-es.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Draw it out more.

dialogue.speaker: Mac

dialogue.speaker: Dennis

jokes.text: A pair of poofs about town. Sodomites in frilly lace.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Mac in British disguises
jokes.punchline: Patriots immediately identifying them as gay British stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Shows their disguise backfiring but relies on period-appropriate homophobia

dialogue.line: A pair of poofs about town. Sodomites in frilly lace.

jokes.text: I'm not your slave anymore, Franklin. I'm about to go live like the goddamn Queen of England and never see you two bitches again.
jokes.setup: Dee preparing to marry Cricket for his wealth
jokes.punchline: Her delusions of grandeur and dramatic farewell to the gang
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's materialism and dramatic flair for overdoing declarations

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm not your slave anymore, Franklin. I'm about to go live like the goddamn Queen of England and never see you two bitches again.

jokes.text: so we got tarred and feathered for being Sodomites! It is not a she-male thing...
jokes.setup: Mac explaining what happened to them
jokes.punchline: Dennis still defending his drag disguise despite the consequences
jokes.explanation: Dennis's vanity preventing him from learning from consequences

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: so we got tarred and feathered for being Sodomites!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It is not a she-male thing...

jokes.text: No, I'm unscathed, my darling. This is the blood of my men. We've suffered too many casualties. All our guns jammed.
jokes.setup: Cricket covered in blood after battle
jokes.punchline: Revealing all British guns jammed, connecting to Dee's 'curse'
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes Dee's sarcastic curse seem to have worked

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: No, I'm unscathed, my darling. This is the blood of my men. We've suffered too many casualties. All our guns jammed.

jokes.text: Shit! Goddamn it, Frank. You just ruined my all life. Wait, wait, wait, check his pulse. He doesn't have a head.
jokes.setup: Charlie accidentally decapitating Cricket with bell
jokes.punchline: Charlie suggesting to check the pulse of a headless body
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - suggesting medical check on obviously dead person

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Shit! Goddamn it, Frank. You just ruined my all life. Wait, wait, wait, check his pulse. He doesn't have a head.

jokes.text: Shit! Bro, we just broke the liberty bell. I'll get you, my pretty!
jokes.setup: Trying to dispose of Cricket's body using the Liberty Bell
jokes.punchline: Accidentally cracking the bell and Dee's witch reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the episode's premise - their stupidity actually creates historical event

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Shit! Bro, we just broke the liberty bell.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'll get you, my pretty!

jokes.text: Maybe she didn't understand the story. Maybe skim too much. Yeah, let's tell the false story this time.
jokes.setup: Historical society worker rejecting their story
jokes.punchline: Thinking the problem was they told the TRUE version instead of a fake one
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - their complete misunderstanding of why their story was rejected

dialogue.line: Maybe she didn't understand the story.
Maybe skim too much.
Yeah, let's tell the false story this time.
